PPF Calculator: ₹70K/Year for 20 Years

Invest ₹70K yearly in PPF at 7.1% and get ₹31,07,199 tax-free at maturity. That's ₹17,07,199 in interest earned.

PPF Investment of ₹70K/Year for 20 Years

Current Interest Rate
7.1% p.a.
Lock-in Period
15 Years
Min Deposit/Year
₹500
Max Deposit/Year
₹1,50,000
Tax Benefit
EEE (Exempt-Exempt-Exempt)
Section 80C Limit
₹1,50,000

If you invest ₹70K per year (approximately ₹5,833/month) in PPF at the current rate of 7.1%, your investment will grow to ₹31,07,199 in 20 years.

  • Total amount deposited: ₹14,00,000
  • Total interest earned (tax-free): ₹17,07,199
  • Effective wealth multiplier: 2.22x
  • Estimated annual tax savings (30% bracket): ₹21,840

Frequently Asked Questions

What is the maturity value of ₹70,000 yearly PPF investment for 20 years?

If you invest ₹70,000 per year in PPF at 7.1% for 20 years, your maturity value will be ₹31,07,199. This includes ₹14,00,000 total deposits and ₹17,07,199 in tax-free interest.

How much tax can I save with ₹70,000 PPF investment?

Deposits up to ₹1,50,000 per year qualify for Section 80C deduction. If you are in the 31.2% tax bracket (highest old regime), you can save approximately ₹21,840 in taxes every year.

How much should I invest monthly to deposit ₹70,000 per year in PPF?

To invest ₹70,000 per year in PPF, you need to set aside approximately ₹5,833 per month. You can make deposits in up to 12 installments per financial year.

What happens after 20 years PPF maturity?

A 20-year PPF investment means extending beyond the initial 15-year lock-in in 5-year blocks. After maturity, you can withdraw the full amount tax-free or continue extending in 5-year blocks.

Is PPF investment of ₹70,000/year better than FD?

At 7.1% tax-free return, PPF significantly outperforms FDs for long-term investment. A comparable FD would need to offer 10.3% pre-tax returns (for 31.2% tax bracket) to match PPF's after-tax return. Over 20 years, this compounding advantage is substantial.

PPF vs FD6.5 - 7.5% | Low riskPPF vs ELSS12 - 15 (historical average)% | High (equity market linked) riskPPF vs NPS8 - 10 (historical average)% | Moderate (mix of equity, debt, government securities) riskPPF vs SSA8.2 (current rate)% | Zero (Government backed) riskPPF vs EPF8.25 (FY 2023-24)% | Zero (Government backed) riskPPF vs NSC7.7 (current rate)% | Zero (Government backed) riskPPF vs RD6.5 - 7.0% | Low risk

PPF calculations are based on the current interest rate of 7.1% p.a. compounded annually. Actual returns may vary based on government rate revisions.
